mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.33% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained FEDFUNDS above 4.0% signals continued policy tightness that keeps real yields elevated and prevents Treasury yield compression. Over the next 20 trading days, the 10-year yield remains anchored upward as the market prices in persistent rate maintenance.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining above 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.1% combined with PAYEMS at 159,218 thousand indicates a tight labor market. If UNRATE rises above 4.5% within the next 60 trading days, it signals labor market deterioration that triggers flight-to-safety demand for Treasuries, compressing the 10-year yield downward as growth expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% or PAYEMS declines by more than 100 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises despite labor market deterioration signal, or the correlation between UNRATE increases and subsequent 60-day Treasury yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real sales (RSAFS at 723,853 million) and housing starts (HOUST at 1,366 thousand) reflect consumer demand strength. Sustained growth in RSAFS above trend signals robust aggregate demand that pressures inflation expectations and forces the 10-year yield upward over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices terminal rate expectations.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls despite RSAFS remaining elevated, or month-over-month RSAFS growth does not correlate positively with subsequent 20-day yield changes (Spearman rho < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 103.511 (2017=100) combined with inflation (CPIAUCSL at 319.775) shows supply-side constraints persisting. If INDPRO declines below 102.5 while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, stagflation signals emerge that force the 10-year yield higher as real rate expectations compress and inflation risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 102.5 while CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.3%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls when stagflation signal triggers, or the joint effect of INDPRO decline and CPIAUCSL elevation does not produce statistically significant yield increases (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1,366 thousand) represent forward-looking demand expectations. A decline in HOUST below 1,200 thousand signals weakening housing demand that portends broader economic slowdown, triggering Treasury demand and compressing the 10-year yield downward within 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,200 thousand in any single month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises following HOUST decline below 1,200 thousand, or the average 5-day yield change following such declines is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL at 319.775) combined with FEDFUNDS at 4.33% implies a real federal funds rate near 1.1%. If CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth accelerates above 0.4% while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged, the market reprices expectations for future rate hikes, pushing the 10-year yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4% while FEDFUNDS remains within 10 basis points of prior month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls despite inflation acceleration and unchanged policy rates, or the correlation between unexpected CPIAUCSL increases and subsequent 20-day yield changes is not significantly positive (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 159,218 thousand) at elevated levels combined with unemployment at 4.1% indicates a tight labor market. Sustained PAYEMS growth above 150 thousand month-over-month signals persistent wage pressure that forces the 10-year yield upward as the market prices in higher-for-longer inflation and policy rates over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls despite sustained strong PAYEMS growth, or the rolling 3-month correlation between PAYEMS growth and subsequent 60-day yield changes is not significantly positive (Spearman rho < 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 23,536.293 billion chained 2017 dollars) combined with retail sales (RSAFS at 723,853 million) and housing (HOUST at 1,366 thousand) form a demand composite. If all three series show simultaneous weakness—GDPC1 growth slowing below 1.5% annualized, RSAFS declining month-over-month, and HOUST below 1,250 thousand—a synchronized demand shock triggers flight-to-safety Treasury buying, compressing the 10-year yield downward within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 1,250 thousand in the same month
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock or recession onset
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises despite coordinated demand weakness signal, or the joint effect of RSAFS decline and HOUST weakness does not produce statistically significant yield compression (t-test p > 0.15).
